title: Women Power Day Around the World
story: Purple fills the streets as millions celebrate International Women's Day on March 8! People wear purple ribbons, share stories of amazing women, and march together for equal rights. Schools teach about female scientists, leaders, and artists who changed history. Social media explodes with #WomenSupportingWomen posts. Many countries give women flowers or small gifts to show appreciation for their contributions to families and communities.
activity_question: What color do people commonly wear to celebrate International Women's Day?
bonus_fact: Over 100 countries officially recognize International Women's Day as a national holiday.

title: Nutty Clusters Get Their Crunchy Day
story: Crunchy peanuts covered in sweet chocolate create the perfect treat on March 8 for National Peanut Cluster Day! These bumpy, delicious candies look like tiny chocolate mountains with nuts poking out everywhere. People celebrate by making homemade clusters in their kitchens, melting chocolate and stirring in roasted peanuts. Candy stores often put peanut clusters on sale, and families share recipes that have been passed down for years. Some folks even host cluster-making parties where everyone gets messy hands from dipping and rolling the treats.
activity_question: What do peanut clusters look like according to the story?
activity_choices: Tiny chocolate mountains with nuts poking out
activity_answer: Tiny chocolate mountains with nuts poking out
bonus_fact: The first peanut clusters were accidentally created when nuts fell into melting chocolate!
